title: Modern JavaScript: Runtime to Accessible Application

audience.description: Learners moving from HTML and CSS into rigorous modern JavaScript application development.
entryKnowledge: Semantic HTML, accessible controls, responsive CSS, browser files, and basic developer tools.
deviceConstraints: Modern browser with workers enabled; all required JavaScript execution occurs locally in disposable browser workers.
accessibilityAssumptions: Every interaction supports keyboard navigation, zoom, readable text output, reduced motion, and assistive technology.

placementEvidence: Build and explain a semantic responsive page with accessible controls and browser developer-tool evidence.
completionEvidence: Defend four cumulative JavaScript projects against changed inputs and accessibility requirements.
Pass the cumulative performance exam at or above 85 percent.

includes: Asynchronous JavaScript, modules, DOM, accessible interaction, web data, security, testing, and release
excludes: Production HTTP servers, taught in later TypeScript and backend courses

sources.title: MDN JavaScript Guide
sources.scope: Controls language sequence, collections, objects, promises, modules, resource management, and advanced concepts.

sources.title: ECMAScript 2026 Language Specification
sources.scope: Controls runtime semantics, types, expressions, execution contexts, jobs, modules, and language accuracy.

sources.title: MDN Web API Reference
sources.scope: Controls DOM, events, workers, fetch, storage, abort, and browser security boundary coverage.

sources.title: WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices Guide
sources.scope: Controls keyboard, focus, status update, and accessible interaction requirements.
